# The Hitchhiker's Practical Guide to Improbability Drives

The Infinite Improbability Drive is perhaps the most bewildering piece of technology ever to confuse a sentient being, which is saying quite a lot when you consider that sentient beings are easily confused by things like automatic doors and tax forms.

The drive works by harnessing the power of quantum improbability to transport a vessel across vast distances instantaneously. In practical terms, this means that the ship passes through every conceivable point in every conceivable universe before arriving at the desired destination. Side effects may include temporary transformation into a penguin, spontaneous generation of petunias, and an overwhelming desire to have a cup of tea.

The key breakthrough in Improbability Drive technology came when a student at the University of Maximegalon realized that the probability of the drive being invented was precisely the same as the probability of a chimpanzee spontaneously typing out the complete works of Shakespeare. Since this had, in fact, already happened (in the back room of an infinite number of libraries), the drive essentially invented itself.

Operating manual excerpt: "Do not attempt to calculate the odds of any event while the drive is engaged. The mathematics involved have been known to cause permanent cross-eyedness and an irrational fear of the number 42."
